title: The Adventures of Sherlock Holmes
summary: Sherlock Holmes gets a mysterious letter about a big problem.
coverScene: Sherlock Holmes sitting in his armchair, surrounded by books and papers, with a magnifying glass in hand.

sentences.text: It was a cold winter evening when I visited my friend, Sherlock Holmes.
The streets of London were covered in thick white snow.
I walked to his house and went up the stairs to his room.
He was sitting in his usual chair by the warm fire, looking very tired.
A small light was burning on the table beside him.
His face was pale, and he had been thinking deeply for many hours.
I could see that he needed a new problem to focus his mind.
But when I told him that I was back in London, he became much happier.
His dark eyes shone with a sudden light, and he smiled.
He immediately asked me about my life and what I had been doing.
I told him about my busy work as a doctor, which took up most of my time.
I also spoke about my new wife, who made me very happy.
He listened to everything with great interest.
He looked at me carefully for a few moments.
Then he said that I looked both happy and healthy.
He also noticed some wet ground on my boots, which showed that I had been walking in the rain.
He said that if I had not been working so hard, my boots would be cleaner.
Then he started to talk about his own life.
He explained that he had been working on several difficult cases.
He told me that he was still very interested in crime and his special work.
I was amazed by his extraordinary ability to notice small details.
He could easily see things that other people missed.
If he looked at a man, he could tell their whole story.
He could use those small details to solve the most difficult mysteries.
He showed me a letter which had been received by him earlier that day.
The letter was written on very strong paper that came from a foreign country.
Holmes explained that the paper itself was an important sign.
He said that if we examined the paper, we would find its origin.
The person who wrote the letter did not give their name.
However, the writer said they had a very important problem to discuss.
The letter was written in a strange way, which made Holmes very interested.
He thought it might be a very interesting case to solve.
He asked me if I would stay and help him with it.
I agreed to stay because I always enjoyed our work together.
We waited patiently for the mysterious person to arrive.
The fire was burning quietly, and the room was very still.
Finally, at exactly seven forty-five in the evening, we heard a vehicle stop outside.
A moment later, the door was opened, and our visitor entered.
They were dressed in a long coat and a dark hat.
The visitor looked very nervous and worried as they stood before us.
Their face was partly covered by their hat, which made them look even more strange.
Holmes greeted them warmly and asked them to sit down in a comfortable chair.
He told them that they could speak freely in front of me.
He said that I was his most trusted friend.
The person began to explain their situation, and we listened carefully.
They spoke about a secret relationship that had occurred some years ago.
A photograph had been taken of them with another person, and now they were being threatened.
The photograph was very important because it could ruin their reputation.
They really needed Holmes to find this photograph and save them.
As the visitor spoke, Holmes took detailed notes and asked many questions.
He was extremely focused and did not miss a single word.
I could see that his brilliant mind was already working on a plan.
He was a very smart man, and I was happy to be involved in this search.
I knew that we would do everything possible to help our visitor.
Our investigation began the very next morning.
We had to wear different clothes so that people would not recognize us.
Holmes was a master of using different appearances to gather information.
We went to the house where the photograph was believed to be hidden.
We watched the building carefully for several hours to learn the habits of the owner.
Holmes created a smart plan to enter the house.
He said that if we caused a small accident, we would be able to get inside.
I was told to throw a small object through the window when he gave the sign.
This would create a sudden cry of fire, which would frighten the people in the house.
The plan was carried out perfectly.
When the cry of fire was heard, the woman who lived there ran to save her most valuable thing.
In moments of danger, a person will always look at what they want to protect most.
This action showed Holmes exactly where the secret photograph was hidden.
He did not take it immediately, but we now knew its location.
The next day, we returned to the house to get the photograph.
However, when we arrived, we discovered that the woman had already left the country.
She had realized that Holmes was investigating her.
She left a different photograph of herself and a letter for Holmes.
In her letter, she promised that she would never use the secret photograph against our visitor.
We immediately informed our visitor about what had happened.
He was very happy with this result because he knew he was finally safe.
He thanked Holmes and me warmly for our help.
Although we did not get the original photograph, the danger was completely gone.
The visitor was able to return to his life without any more fear.
It was a very good experience for me.
I was glad to have been able to work with Holmes on such an interesting case.
I learned a lot from him during our time together.
He was a very smart man who had a unique way of looking at the world.
I was happy to have him as my friend, and I knew I would never forget our adventures.

quiz.q: What was the weather like when the narrator visited Sherlock Holmes?
What did the narrator tell Holmes about his life?
options: He was moving to a new city
He was back in London and working as a doctor

quiz.q: Why did the mysterious person want to meet with Holmes?
options: To ask for his help with a case

sentences.text: It was a very cold winter evening.
I decided to visit my dear friend, Sherlock Holmes.
I walked through the dark streets of London to reach his house.
When I entered, he was sitting in his large chair.
He looked very tired from his work.
However, I told him that I was back in London.
As soon as he heard this, he became much more active.
He seemed very happy to see me after such a long time.
Holmes immediately began to ask me about my life.
He wanted to know what I had been doing recently.
I told him about my busy work as a doctor.
I also told him about my wonderful new wife.
He listened to my words very carefully.
After I finished, he looked at me with a warm smile.
He said that I looked very happy and healthy.
He was glad that my life was going so well.
Then, Holmes started to talk about his own life.
He had been working on some very difficult cases recently.
He told me that his work was the most important thing to him.
He was still extremely interested in crime and solving mysteries.
If he does not have a difficult problem, he gets bored.
He needs to use his mind all the time.
He said that the city was full of mysteries.
I was always amazed by his wonderful ability.
He could notice the smallest details about a person or a place.
He saw things that other people completely missed.
Then, he used those details to solve the most difficult mysteries.
I have seen him do this many times before.
Yet, it always surprised me.
He could look at a coat and know where the person had been.
Holmes reached into his pocket and took out a letter.
He showed it to me and asked me to read it.
The letter had arrived earlier that day.
It was from someone who wanted to meet with him tonight.
The writer did not give their name in the letter.
However, they wrote that they had a very important problem to discuss.
It was clear that the writer was important.
Holmes was very interested in this strange letter.
He thought it might become a highly interesting case for him.
He asked me to stay and help him with this new mystery.
He said, "If you stay, we will solve this together."
I was very excited by his offer.
I had missed our adventures together.
It felt wonderful to be ready to work with him again.
I quickly agreed to stay and help him.
We decided to wait together for the strange person to arrive.
We sat by the warm fire and talked.
I knew that when the guest arrived, we would start our new investigation.
The clock on the wall made a slow, quiet sound.
We both looked at the door, expecting to hear a knock at any moment.
Finally, the person arrived at exactly seven forty-five in the evening.
We heard a horse stop outside the house.
Then, there was a loud knock on the door.
The guest entered our room wearing a long coat and a hat.
The person looked very afraid and worried.
They looked around the room quickly, as if they were afraid of something.
Holmes welcomed the visitor warmly and asked them to sit down.
He tried to make the guest feel more comfortable in the room.
Then, he asked them to explain their difficult problem to us.
The person began to talk in a worried voice.
We both sat quietly and listened very carefully to every word they said.
As the visitor spoke, Holmes took notes on a piece of paper.
He also asked a few sharp questions to understand the details.
He was extremely focused and concentrated on the story.
I could see that his active mind was already working.
He was already thinking about how to solve this difficult case.
This was the beginning of another great adventure.

quiz.q: What was Holmes doing when the narrator first arrived?
options: He was writing a letter to a friend.
He was sitting in a large chair, looking tired.
He was walking around the streets of London.

quiz.q: What did Holmes show the narrator?
options: A book about his past cases.
A letter from an unnamed writer.

quiz.q: What time did the visitor arrive at the house?
options: At eight o'clock in the evening.
